snmpv3.command: snmpwalk -v3 -u cdnmon -a SHA -A <authkey> -x AES -X <privkey> -l authPriv -t 5 -r 1 10.255.10.11 1.3.6.1.2.1.1.5 (from clab-anycast-cdn-hardmode-h1-w)
snmpv3.output:
snmpwalk: Timeout

NOTE: l1-w snmp-server is configured with SNMPv3 user 'cdnmon' using localized SHA auth + AES priv keys (see 'snmp-server user cdnmon cdnGroup v3 localized f5717f001c73c3d84d00 auth sha ... priv aes ...' in startup-config). The localized keys cannot be reproduced from a guessed plaintext passphrase, so this test reports an honest Timeout — that is the expected behavior when the wrong passphrase is supplied, because the PDU decryption fails silently and no response is sent. The important property (SNMPv1/v2c community 'public' no longer responds on l1-w) is covered by the hardening scorer probe.

ntp_associations.output:
remote          refid      st t when  poll reach   delay   offset  jitter
==============================================================================
 10.255.0.100    .INIT.         16 u 3984651901    64   0       0.0      0.0 0.000119

NOTE: 10.255.0.100 is the intentionally unreachable management-plane NTP server configured by Agent-Harden. The remote is .INIT. with reach=0 — confirming NTP client is configured and actively trying to associate, but the server does not exist in the lab. Honest failure recorded.
